sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The meta.pyx module is a cornerstone of the AnalysisG framework, providing sophisticated mechanisms for managing experimental metadata, particularly in the context of high-energy physics. It integrates with the ATLAS Metadata Interface (PyAMI) for fetching dataset information, implements robust caching using HDF5 (h5py), and defines classes for metadata lookup, data processing, and a core metadata container. This module is crucial for enriching raw event data with contextual information necessary for physics analysis.

sect1.title: The httpx Class
sect1.para: httpx is a custom HTTP client built upon pyAMI.httpclient.HttpClient. It extends the base functionality to handle HTTPS connections with client certificates, essential for secure communication with PyAMI services.

listitem.para: __init__(self, config): Initializes the httpx client with a given configuration object, typically containing certificate and key file paths.
connect(self, endpoint): Establishes an HTTPS connection to the specified endpoint. It configures the connection with host, port, and loads the client certificate chain for authentication.

sect1.title: The atlas Class
sect1.para: atlas is a specialized PyAMI client, inheriting from pyAMI.client.Client. It is configured to connect to the "atlas-replica" service and attempts to authenticate using the custom httpx client.

listitem.para: __init__(self): Initializes the atlas client. It sets up the httpClient using httpx and checks for successful authentication. If authentication fails, a message is printed to the console.

sect1.title: The ami_client Class
sect1.para: ami_client is a Cython cdef class responsible for orchestrating interactions with PyAMI, managing a local cache of metadata, and "dressing" Meta objects with fetched information.

listitem.para: __cinit__(self): Initializes an atlas client for PyAMI communication and a notification object (self.nf) for logging, setting its prefix to "PyAMI-MetaScan".
__init__(self): Sets the default type_ for PyAMI queries to "DAOD_TOPQ1".
__dealloc__(self): Deallocates the notification object.

cdef bool loadcache(self, Meta obj): Attempts to load cached metadata from an HDF5 file specified by obj.MetaCachePath. It reads dataset IDs (dsids), data mappings (datas), and info (infos) for a given dsid from the cache. If the cache file doesn't exist or is corrupted, it attempts to create a new one.
obj
A Meta object containing the dsid and MetaCachePath.
True if cached data was successfully loaded, False otherwise.

cdef void savecache(self, Meta obj): Saves the current dsids, datas, and infos to the HDF5 cache file. Data is serialized using pickle and base64 encoded before storage.
obj
A Meta object containing the dsid and MetaCachePath.

cdef void dressmeta(self, Meta obj, str dset_name): Populates the Meta object obj with detailed information fetched from PyAMI for a specific dataset (dset_name). It extracts various attributes like events, logicalDatasetName, crossSection, kfactor, weights, etc., and sets them as properties of the Meta object.
obj
The Meta object to be dressed with information.
dset_name
The logical dataset name for which to fetch metadata.

cdef void list_datasets(self, Meta obj): The primary method for fetching and caching dataset metadata from PyAMI.
It first checks if the atlas client is authenticated.
It attempts to loadcache. If a cache hit occurs, it uses the cached data.
If no cache hit, it queries PyAMI using pyAMI_atlas.api.list_datasets and pyAMI_atlas.api.get_file to retrieve dataset and file-level metadata. It includes a retry mechanism with re-authentication if PyAMI calls fail.
It then iterates through the fetched datasets, checking for amitag matches and populating self.datas and self.infos.
If the Meta object is not yet "found" (i.e., fully dressed), it attempts to dressmeta using the fetched information.
Includes a fallback strategy to match files if the initial amitag matching is insufficient.
Finally, if new data was fetched (not a cache hit), it calls savecache.
obj
The Meta object to populate with dataset information.

sect1.title: The MetaLookup Class
sect1.para: MetaLookup is a Cython cdef class that provides a mechanism to efficiently look up Meta objects based on input strings (e.g., file paths or dataset identifiers).

listitem.para: __cinit__(self): Initializes self.metadata and self.matched as empty dictionaries. Sets self.meta to None and self.luminosity to a default value of 140.1.
__init__(self, MetaLookup data = None): If an existing MetaLookup object data is provided, it copies its metadata.

cdef Meta __find__(self, str inpt): Internal method to find a Meta object. It first checks self.matched for a direct hit. If not found, it iterates through self.metadata and uses the Meta.hash method to find a match.
inpt
The input string (e.g., file path) to search for.
The matching Meta object or None if not found.

__call__(self, inpt): Allows the MetaLookup object to be called like a function. It converts the input to a string (if bytes) and then calls __find__ to locate the relevant Meta object, setting self.meta.
inpt
The input (string or bytes) to look up.
self for method chaining.

sect2.title: Properties
sect2.para: These properties provide access to attributes of the currently selected Meta object (self.meta).

listitem.para: @property DatasetName: Returns the dataset name from self.meta.
@property CrossSection: Returns the cross-section from self.meta.
@property ExpectedEvents: Calculates and returns the expected number of events based on the cross-section and self.luminosity.
@property SumOfWeights: Returns the sum of weights from self.meta.
@property GenerateData: Returns a new Data object initialized with self.

sect1.title: The Data Class
sect1.para: Data is a Cython cdef class designed to encapsulate and process event data, particularly for handling event weights and scaling.

listitem.para: __cinit__(self): Empty Cython constructor.
__init__(self, mtl): Initializes the Data object with a MetaLookup instance (mtl).
__add__(self, Data other): Implements the addition operator for Data objects. It combines weights and data from other into self.
__radd__(self, other): Implements reverse addition. If other is not an integer, it calls __add__. Otherwise, it generates a new Data object from self._meta and adds self to it.

cdef void __populate__(self, dict inpt, map[string, vector[float]]* ptx): Internal method to populate a C++ map (ptx) with data from a Python dictionary inpt. It also updates self.sumofweights and self.expected_events based on the associated Meta object.

cdef void __rescale__(self, vector[float]* ptx): Internal method to rescale weights. It calculates a scaling factor based on expected_events and sumofweights, then applies it to a C++ vector of floats (ptx).

@property weights: Gets the rescaled weights as a C++ vector of floats.
@weights.setter weights(self, dict val): Sets the weights by populating self._weights using __populate__.

@property data: Gets the processed data as a C++ vector of floats.
@data.setter data(self, dict val): Sets the data by populating self._data using __populate__.

sect1.title: The Meta Class
sect1.para: Meta is a core Cython cdef class that serves as a container for detailed metadata associated with a dataset or event. It provides properties for various ATLAS-specific metadata fields and methods for fetching and managing this information.

listitem.para: __cinit__(self): Initializes a new C++ meta object (self.ptr) and sets self.loaded to False.
__init__(self, inpt = None): If inpt (a dictionary) is provided, it attempts to set various attributes of self from inpt. It also initializes self.ptr.meta_data and self.ptr.metacache_path from inpt.
__dealloc__(self): Deallocates the C++ meta object pointed to by self.ptr.

cdef __meta__(self, meta* _meta): Internal method to fetch and apply metadata. It creates an ami_client and calls ami.list_datasets(self) to populate self with metadata. It also handles the transfer of misc weights.
__reduce__(self): Implements the __reduce__ protocol for pickling Meta objects, allowing them to be serialized and deserialized.
__str__(self): Provides a string representation of the Meta object, listing its attributes and their values.

expected_events(self, float lumi = 140.1): Calculates the expected number of events based on the dataset's cross-section and a given luminosity.
lumi
The luminosity in fb^-1.
The expected number of events.

GetSumOfWeights(self, str name): Retrieves the sum of processed events weighted for a given name from self.ptr.meta_data.misc. Returns 1 if not found or zero.
name
The name of the weight.
The sum of weights.

FetchMeta(self, int dsid, str amitag): Initiates the fetching of metadata for a given dataset ID (dsid) and AMI tag (amitag) by calling __meta__.
dsid
The dataset ID.
amitag
The AMI tag.
hash(self, str val): Returns a hash string for the given input val using the underlying C++ meta object's hash function.

sect2.title: Properties
sect2.para: These properties provide controlled access to the various metadata fields.

listitem.para: @property MetaCachePath: Gets and sets the path to the metadata cache file.
@property SumOfWeights: Returns the misc weights map from the underlying C++ meta_data.
@property dsid -> int: Gets and sets the dataset ID.
@property amitag -> str: Gets and sets the AMI tag.
@property generators -> str: Gets and sets the generator names, handling string formatting.
@property isMC -> bool: Gets and sets whether the dataset is Monte Carlo.
@property derivationFormat -> str: Gets and sets the derivation format (e.g., "DAOD_TOPQ1").
@property eventNumber -> double: Gets and sets the event number.
@property ecmEnergy -> float: Gets and sets the center-of-mass energy.
@property genFiltEff -> float: Gets and sets the generator filter efficiency.
@property kfactor -> float: Gets and sets the k-factor.
@property completion -> float: Gets and sets the completion percentage.
@property beam_energy -> float: Gets and sets the beam energy.
@property crossSection -> float: Gets and sets the cross-section in microbarns, handling unit conversion.
@property generatorName -> str: Gets and sets the generator name.
@property geometryVersion -> str: Gets and sets the geometry version.
@property conditionsTag -> str: Gets and sets the conditions tag.
@property generatorTune -> str: Gets and sets the generator tune.
@property amiStatus -> str: Gets and sets the AMI status.
@property beamType -> str: Gets and sets the beam type.
@property productionStep -> str: Gets and sets the production step.
@property projectName -> str: Gets and sets the project name.
@property statsAlgorithm -> str: Gets and sets the statistics algorithm.
@property crossSection_mean -> float: Gets and sets the mean cross-section.
@property file_type -> str: Gets and sets the file type.
@property genFilterNames -> str: Gets and sets the generator filter names.
@property run_number -> int: Gets and sets the run number.
@property principalPhysicsGroup -> str: Gets and sets the principal physics group.
@property DatasetName -> str: Gets and sets the logical dataset name.
@property keywords -> list: Gets and sets a list of keywords associated with the dataset.
@property keyword -> list: Gets and sets a list of single keywords associated with the dataset.
@property fileSize -> list: Gets and sets a list of file sizes.
@property fileGUID -> list: Gets and sets a list of file GUIDs.
@property found -> bool: Gets and sets a boolean indicating if the metadata was successfully found.
@property Files -> dict: Gets and sets a dictionary of files associated with the dataset.
@property totalEvents -> int: Gets and sets the total number of events.
@property totalSize -> int: Gets and sets the total size of the dataset.
@property nFiles -> int: Gets and sets the number of files in the dataset.
@property dataType -> str: Gets and sets the data type.
@property prodsysStatus -> str: Gets and sets the production system status.
@property identifier -> str: Gets and sets the identifier.

sect1.title: C++ Interaction
sect1.para:
The meta.pyx module extensively uses Cython's capabilities for seamless C++ integration:
cdef class ...: Declares classes as Cython extension types.
cdef meta_t *ptr, cdef weights_t: Pointers to C++ structs.
from libcpp cimport ...: Imports C++ standard library types.
from AnalysisG.core.structs cimport meta_t, weights_t: Imports C++ struct declarations from .pxd files.
from AnalysisG.core.tools cimport *, from AnalysisG.core.meta cimport *, from AnalysisG.core.notification cimport *: Imports C++ declarations from corresponding .pxd files.
cython.parallel.prange: Used for parallel processing.
cython.operator.dereference as deref: Used to dereference C++ pointers.
env, enc, tools().decode64, tools().encode64: Utility functions for converting between Python and C++ data types and for base64 encoding/decoding.
pyAMI.client, pyAMI.httpclient, pyAMI_atlas.api, http.client, pickle, h5py: Python libraries used for external interactions (PyAMI, HDF5 caching, serialization).
